questions.question: What is the main purpose of Gemini Notebook?
choices: To browse the open web and gather information from any source
To work exclusively from sources you provide, synthesizing them without outside context
To replace traditional note-taking apps with AI-powered alternatives
To generate content without requiring any user input or direction
questions.feedback_correct: Exactly—Gemini Notebook is designed to work only with material you give it, which keeps the output grounded and traceable.
questions.feedback_incorrect: Gemini Notebook actually does not browse the web or pull in outside context. It works exclusively from the sources you provide, which is a feature that ensures grounded, traceable output.

questions.question: What is Studio within Gemini Notebook?
choices: A separate application that requires its own login
The panel inside every notebook where you generate rich content formats
A feature that only works with video files
A collaboration tool for sharing notebooks with other users
questions.feedback_correct: That's right—Studio is the content generation hub where you turn your sources into formats like Audio Overviews and Video Overviews.
questions.feedback_incorrect: Studio is a panel within Gemini Notebook itself, not a separate application. It's where you create content formats from your sources.

questions.question: What is a Video Overview in the context of this course?
choices: A raw, unstructured recording of everything in your source material
A structured, presenter-style summary built directly from source material
An interactive quiz format that tests comprehension
A live-streamed presentation that requires audience participation
questions.feedback_correct: Correct—a Video Overview is a structured briefing format, presenter-style, designed to help someone get up to speed quickly on source material.
questions.feedback_incorrect: A Video Overview is not raw or unstructured. It's a polished, organized summary delivered in a presenter style, perfect for quick briefings before meetings or for colleagues who need to catch up fast.

questions.question: In the hands-on exercise, what is the first action you take after signing into Gemini Notebook?
choices: Add a website source immediately
Open Studio and select Video Overview
Create a new notebook and give it a name
Watch an instructional video about the interface
questions.feedback_correct: Yes—after signing in, the first step is creating a new notebook with a meaningful name for your workspace.
questions.feedback_incorrect: While you do need to sign in first, the next step is to create a new notebook. This gives you your private workspace before you add any sources.

questions.question: What should you look for in the Sources panel to confirm that Gemini Notebook has finished processing a website you added?
choices: A green checkmark icon next to the URL
A notification popup thanking you for adding the source
The source appears with a title and short summary beneath it
An email confirmation sent to your Google account
questions.feedback_correct: Exactly—when the source appears in the list with a title pulled from the page and a summary underneath, that's your confirmation it's ready to use.
questions.feedback_incorrect: Gemini Notebook signals completion by displaying the source with a title and summary in your Sources panel. This is what tells you the AI has processed your material and is ready to work with it.

questions.question: What happens when you click Generate for a Video Overview in Studio?
choices: The video appears instantly on your screen
Gemini Notebook builds a structured video summary from your source material, which takes a few seconds to a couple of minutes
You are prompted to record your own voiceover
The tool opens your source in a video editor for you to manually create the overview
questions.feedback_correct: That's correct—generating a Video Overview takes time because Gemini Notebook is actively synthesizing and structuring your source material into that format.
questions.feedback_incorrect: Video Overview generation is not instant, but it is automated. Gemini Notebook does the work of building the structured summary for you from your source—no manual editing or recording required.

questions.question: According to the course, why is the grounded nature of Gemini Notebook's output described as important?
choices: It allows you to generate content faster than other tools
It ensures every insight can be traced back to source material you provided, not invented information
It prevents other users from seeing your work
It requires less storage space on your device
questions.feedback_correct: Precisely—because Gemini Notebook works only from your sources, you can verify where every piece of information came from, which is a significant advantage.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The grounded approach is valuable because it means the output is traceable and fact-based. You're not getting hallucinated or outside information mixed in—only what you actually provided.

questions.question: After completing the hands-on exercise, what option is offered to you before closing out?
choices: A survey asking for feedback on the course
A written summary document of every step you completed that you can reuse
A certification badge for finishing the exercise
A link to purchase Gemini Notebook premium features
questions.feedback_correct: Right—Gemini Notebook can create a clean, reusable reference document summarizing the steps you just completed.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The course offers you a written reference document of the steps you performed so you can repeat the process independently on your own material later.

questions.question: Based on the course content, what would be the most appropriate next step after completing this training?
choices: Wait for an email with advanced features to unlock
Open a new notebook this week with actual material from your work and run the same steps
Take the course again with a different website URL
Sign up for a Gemini Notebook certification program
questions.feedback_correct: Exactly—the course explicitly recommends applying what you learned to real work material this week, which is where the tool becomes genuinely useful.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The point of this training is not just to learn the steps once, but to apply them to your own work. Taking what you've practiced with a random webpage and using it on actual documents, reports, or materials from your job is how you'll see real value.

questions.question: Why does the course emphasize that Gemini Notebook works only from sources you provide rather than browsing the open web?
choices: Because web browsing would make the tool too slow to use
Because it is a limitation that Google will eventually fix in future updates
Because it ensures output is grounded, traceable, and based only on material you control
Because web sources are less reliable than the sources you choose
questions.feedback_correct: Perfect—this design choice is presented as a feature, not a limitation, because it guarantees that everything generated comes from your actual material.
questions.feedback_incorrect: The closed-source approach is actually presented as a strength. By working only from what you provide, the tool ensures every output is grounded in material you control and can verify, rather than pulling in unpredictable outside context.
